reason: The text demonstrates the opposite relationship. The Israelites consistently obey their leaders Moses and Aaron throughout the passage. Key evidence includes: (1) 'וּבְנֵי־ יִשְׂרָאֵ֥ל עָשׂ֖וּ כִּדְבַ֣ר מֹשֶׁ֑ה' - 'the children of Israel did according to the word of Moses'; (2) the people comply with instructions regarding the Passover; (3) 'וַיַּֽעֲשׂ֖וּ כָּל־ בְּנֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל כַּאֲשֶׁ֨ר צִוָּ֧ה יְהוָ֛ה אֶת־ מֹשֶׁ֥ה וְאֶֽת־ אַהֲרֹ֖ן' - 'all the children of Israel did as the Lord commanded Moses and Aaron.' The passage portrays a relationship of obedience and compliance, not antagonism.
